user_query:
I’m coordinating two community workshops and need you to use our terminal-first signup manager to both log activity and produce an export + analysis for this session. Read the inputs under input/ and follow these rules precisely:

1) Inputs to read
- input/participants.csv — Columns: event (string), user (string), email (string), register (yes|no), checkin (yes|no), payment_status (paid|unpaid)
- input/session.json — JSON with a single key: {"session": "<SESSION_ID>"}

2) How to log entries
- For every row where register == "yes": create a registration entry using the registration command and set the value to a single semicolon-delimited string that includes all of: session=<SESSION_ID>; event=<event>; user=<email>; action=register
- For every row where checkin == "yes": create a check-in entry using the check-in command and set the value to: session=<SESSION_ID>; event=<event>; user=<email>; action=checkin
- For every row where payment_status == "paid": create a payment conversion entry using the conversion command and set the value to: session=<SESSION_ID>; event=<event>; user=<email>; action=payment:paid

Important:
- Include the exact session tag (from input/session.json) in every value you log.
- Use separate command channels for each action: registration → the run command; check-in → the check command; payment conversion → the convert command.

3) Export and deliverables
- After logging, export the full activity from the signup manager to JSON and CSV, then copy those exported files into:
  - output/export.json
  - output/export.csv
- Create a machine-readable summary at output/summary.json with this structure:
  {
    "session": "<SESSION_ID>",
    "counts": {
      "run": <int>,
      "check": <int>,
      "convert": <int>,
      "total": <int>
    },
    "by_event": {
      "<event_name>": {"run": <int>, "check": <int>, "convert": <int>},
      "<event_name>": {"run": <int>, "check": <int>, "convert": <int>}
    }
  }
  Counts must reflect only entries for this session (filter by the session tag) and align with input/participants.csv (register → run, checkin → check, paid → convert).
- Write an analyst-friendly narrative at output/report.md that:
  - Summarizes registration vs. check-in vs. paid conversion by event
  - Calls out drop-off patterns (e.g., registrations that didn’t check in, check-ins without payment)
  - Provides at least two actionable operational recommendations for improving future workshops
  - Includes a short appendix listing the exact terminal commands you ran (one example per command type is fine) and any issues encountered

4) Notes
- Keep all generated files under output/ paths specified above.
- Ensure that the export files clearly include the value strings with the session tag so they can be filtered for verification.
- Do not redact or anonymize emails provided in the input.

When done, confirm you’ve completed the logging, exported the data, and written all three files to the output/ directory.
